description: Conversation flow for users experiencing back pain, guiding them through understanding their discomfort and finding sleep solutions.

headerContent.headline: It's Time That You Sleep Better
headerContent.subheadline: Hi, I'm {avatarName}. I help people find relief from {keyword} through better sleep solutions.
headerContent.avatarIntroScript: Welcome! I see you're dealing with {keyword}. You're definitely not alone, and I'm here to help you find the right solution. Let me ask you a few quick questions to understand what you're experiencing.

questionContent.questionText: What kind of discomfort do you notice most when you wake up?
questionContent.helperText: Select the option that best describes your experience

answerOptions.label: Tightness
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Thanks for sharing that. {thisAnswer} is something many people feel when their body hasn't fully relaxed overnight.

answerOptions.label: Sharp pain
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Sharp sensations can feel alarming. A lot of people are surprised when certain sleep habits make this worse.

answerOptions.label: Dull ache
answerOptions.avatarResponse: I hear that often. A dull ache can sometimes be linked to how your body settles during sleep.

answerOptions.label: Stiffness
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Stiffness is one of the most common morning complaints. You're definitely not alone.

answerOptions.label: It changes day to day
answerOptions.avatarResponse: That variation is interesting. It tells me your body might be reacting to different pressures or positions.

questionContent.questionText: When do you feel your {previousAnswerLower} the most during a typical day?

answerOptions.label: First thing in the morning
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Morning discomfort is something I pay extra attention to. It often points to nighttime patterns.

answerOptions.label: Midday
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Midday discomfort can come from a mix of sleep quality and daily habits. Good to know.

answerOptions.label: Evening
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Evening discomfort often builds up throughout the day. Your body might be asking for better recovery time.

answerOptions.label: Throughout the day
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Constant discomfort is tough to deal with. The good news is that better sleep can help your body reset.

questionContent.questionText: How long have you been experiencing this?

answerOptions.label: Less than a week
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Since this is recent, there's a great chance we can help prevent it from becoming a bigger issue.

answerOptions.label: 1-4 weeks
answerOptions.avatarResponse: A few weeks is enough time for your body to develop a pattern. Let's work on breaking that cycle.

answerOptions.label: 1-3 months
answerOptions.avatarResponse: When something lasts a few months, it usually means your body needs consistent support to heal.

answerOptions.label: More than 3 months
answerOptions.avatarResponse: You've been dealing with this for a while. Many of our customers with chronic issues have found real relief with the right sleep setup.

avatarContent.scriptText: Your body does most of its recovery at night. Based on what you've shared about experiencing {step[1].answerLower} especially in the {step[2].answerLower}, I have some ideas that might really help you.

emailCaptureContent.promptText: I'd love to send you personalized recommendations based on your responses. What's the best email to reach you?
emailCaptureContent.avatarResponseOnSubmit: Perfect! I'll send your personalized sleep recommendations to {userEmail}. Now let me show you some products that could help.
emailCaptureContent.avatarResponseOnSkip: No problem! Let me show you some products that could help with your {keyword}.

ctaContent.headline: Your Personalized Sleep Solution
ctaContent.bodyText: Based on your responses about {keyword}, we've identified products that could help you wake up feeling better.
